name: Neil Sedaka
abstract: Neil Sedaka (; March 13, 1939 – February 27, 2026) was an American singer, songwriter, and pianist. Beginning his music career in 1957, he sold millions of records worldwide and wrote or co-wrote over 500 songs for himself and other artists, collaborating mostly with lyricists Howard Greenfield and Phil Cody.

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: And then I found out about demos for songwriters, and it was a wonderful time. There were the older songwriters and there were the new kids coming in like Carole King and Gerry Goffin and Ellie Greenwich and Neil Sedaka, all those people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, Carole King, Neil Sedaka, they worked there. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Only difference is now it’s pot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995
